topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up, a familiar little chime that usually signaled a new assignment, but this time, it was from Mr. Harrison. My breath hitched. I fumbled for my phone, my fingers slick with sudden sweat, and tapped the profile picture. It was him. His face, framed by that slightly too-wide, knowing smile, staring back at me from the screen. A cold wave washed over my stomach, making my hands tremble so violently I had to set the phone down on the desk with a sharp clatter. *No. This isn't happening.* I stared at the screen, my heart hammering against my ribs like a trapped bird. Every nerve ending felt exposed, buzzing with a frantic energy that made my vision swim slightly. I couldn't breathe properly; the air felt too thick, too heavy. I quickly locked the screen, shoving the phone deep into my backpack as if it were radioactive. The silence of the classroom suddenly roared in my ears, amplifying the frantic drumming inside my chest.

[story 2]

I walked into the library feeling like I was walking through quicksand. Every glance felt scrutinized, every shadow seemed to hold a hidden observer. Then I saw it—a familiar blue checkmark next to a post I’d made weeks ago, a post about a particularly baffling historical fact. It was Mr. Harrison. He had tagged me in a photo from a recent school event, a casual inclusion, but now, the sheer proximity of it felt like a physical blow. My mouth went dry. I gripped the edge of the heavy oak table until my knuckles were white, the pressure a desperate anchor against the rising tide of panic. My palms were slick, and I could feel a strange, hot flush creeping up my neck. I wanted to bolt, to disappear into the stacks, but my feet felt rooted to the spot. I kept glancing toward the door, convinced he might materialize, his presence somehow leaking through the screen and into the quiet room.

[story 3]

It started subtly. A like on an old essay, then a comment on a study group thread. I dismissed it as coincidence, a glitch in the algorithm, until I saw the direct message. It was from him. A simple, innocuous question about my weekend plans, yet the message landed in my chest like a punch. My entire body locked up. I stood up so fast my chair scraped loudly against the linoleum floor. I couldn't speak; the sound caught somewhere in my throat, turning into a pathetic squeak. My skin felt too tight, buzzing with an internal static. I paced three steps to the window, my gaze fixed on the quad, searching for any sign of him, any indication that this was some elaborate, cruel prank. The world seemed to tilt on its axis; everything was too bright, too loud, and utterly terrifyingly exposed.
